summary: Completed a visual redesign of the 'How It Works' and 'Problem' landing page sections to establish cohesive aesthetics, resolved merge conflicts with origin/main, fixed code-splitting/ssr build issues, and resolved all Biome lint/format errors.

details: Removed text descriptions from the left selector buttons in how-it-works-section.tsx to balance layout weight.
Added a dynamic vertical timeline progress indicator with a spring-animated glowing tip pointing to the active step.
Moved step-specific descriptions to the right side above an upgraded macOS-style IDE mockup showing code content, file structure, and dynamic state transitions.
Wrapped the component inside how-it-works-client.tsx with 'ssr: false' dynamic loading to bypass Next.js static generation errors caused by client-side animations.
Replaced the static visual layout in problem-section.tsx with 'ChaosVisual', a code-driven live mockup displaying a hallucinating coding assistant and active terminal error logs.
Structured the section header using the same 2-column grid alignment as the HowItWorks header for structural balance.
Refactored the bottom teaser statement into a modern typography display quote with custom scroll-guided elements.
Wrapped inside a dynamic client component to ensure stable client-side animation rendering and build checks.
Merged origin/main into ashwin-dev and successfully resolved merge conflicts in app/globals.css and components/landing/features-section.tsx.
Verified successful local Next.js build compilation after using dynamic code-splitting.
Executed pnpm fix to automatically sort JSX/Framer Motion attributes alphabetically and format styling rules.

Manually resolved residual rules: removed unused index parameter from FeatureCard, added bracket block statements for one-liner conditions in hero-section.tsx/problem-section.tsx, and removed the unused FloatingChip component.
Pushed all clean code changes to remote PR branch ashwin-dev.
